questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In a row of 25 children, Nayan is 14th from the right end. Arun is 3rd to the left of Nayan in the row. What is Arun's position from the left end of the row?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: A class of girls stands in a single line. One girl is 19th in order from both the ends. How many girls are there in the class?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Surbhi ranks 18th in a class of 49 students. What is her rank from the last?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: There are 35 students in a class. Suman ranks third among the girls in the class. Amit ranks 5th among the boys in the class. Suman is one rank below Amit in the class. No, two students hold the same rank in the class. What is Amit's rank in the class?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In a row of children facing North, Ritesh is 12th from the left end. Sudhir, who is 22nd from the right end is 4th to the right of Ritesh. Total how many children are there in the row?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In a class of 45 students, a boy is ranked 20th. When two boys joined, his rank was dropped by one. What is his new rank from the end?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Sohan ranks 7th from the top and 26th from the bottom in a class. How many students are there in the class?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: There are 25 boys in a horizontal row. Rahul was shifted by three places towards his right side and he occupies the middle position of the row. What was his original position from the left end of the row?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In a row of letters, a letter is 5th from left end and 12th from the right end. How many letters are there in a row?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Sunita is the 11th from either end of a row of girls. How many girls are there in that row?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In a row of girls, Veena is 12th from the start and 19th from the end. In another row of girls, Sunita is 14th from the start and 20th from the end. How many girls are there in both the rows together?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Aman is 16th from the left end in a row of boys and Vivek is 18th the from right end. Gagan is 11th from Aman towards the right and 3rd from Vivek towards the right end. How many boys are there in the row?

solutions.solution: (D) Total number of boys = (Number of boys in Jeevan's row) + (Number of boys in Vikas's row) = (6 + 1 + 10) + (9 + 1 + 11) = (17 + 21) = 38
(e) Now, position of Ashwini = (15 + 6) = 21st from left ∴ Ashwini's position from right = (37 + 1 – 21) = 17th

(D) Kamya's new position is 13th from left. But it is the same as Preeti's earlier position which is 6th from the right. The row consists of (12 + 1 + 5) = 18 girls Now, Preeti's new position is Kamya's earlier position which is 5th from the left ∴ Preeti's new position from the right = 18 – 5 + 1
(D) Nayan's position from left = (25 + 1 – 14) = 12 th ∴ Arun's position = (12 – 3) = 9 th from left
(D) Clearly, the girl at the 19th position is exactly in the middle of both the ends. ∴ Total number of girls = (18 + 1 + 18) = 37

(a) Suman ranks 3rd in the class among the girls. Amit ranks 5th among the boys. Suman comes one rank after Amit in the class. It means two girls and four boys rank higher than Amit in the class. So, Amit ranks 7th in the class.
(C) Sudhir's position from left = (12 + 4) = 16 th from left Clearly, there are three students between Ritesh and Sudhir. ∴ Total number of children = (12 + 3 + 22) = 37
